question: Which is true regarding lathe cut silver alloy:

options.B: Achieves lowest compressive strength at 1 hr.
options.C: Has tensile strength both at 15 min & 7 days comparable to high copper unicompositional alloy

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: Lathe-cut silver alloys, due to their composition which includes varying amounts of precious metals like gold and palladium along with other elements such as tin and copper, often have mechanical properties that differ from those containing more mercury. The precise relationship between these variables can influence various aspects including strength development over time and resistance to deformation (creep).

The correct statement appears to be C - it achieves tensile strength levels similar to high-copper unicompsoitional alloys within specified times, suggesting consistent performance characteristics without needing extensive additional processing or material content adjustments compared to alternatives involving higher mercury contents or different compositions.
Answer: C
